meta.description: Use when running prognostic survival analysis on a clinical cohort with time-to-event data to estimate univariate and multivariable Cox proportional hazards models, export result tables, and generate forest plots. NOT for: nomogram construction, calibration curves, time-dependent ROC analysis, or model training/feature selection beyond the built-in univariate screening rule.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics; Cox PH models use standard survival package; HR tables export computed values only; three canonical references cited

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic conclusions; skill is scoped to HR table and forest plot generation; When Not to Use section explicitly excludes clinical decision tools

methodological_ground.detail: Univariate screening with p<0.05 threshold then multivariable model is standard practice; fallback to all features if fewer than 3 significant is appropriate and documented; no causal claims made

code_usability.detail: Script syntax valid; set.seed(options$seed) present; modular 8-file structure; dependency check fires with named packages; no logic errors detected in code review

functional_suitability.note: Three subcommands (analyze, forest-plot, multi-forest-plot) cover full workflow; all documented use cases covered with examples

reliability.note: SKILL_* codes comprehensive; set.seed() present; Cox fitting failure handled with SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER; minor gap: no explicit warning when fallback logic triggers

performance_context.note: SKILL.md is 327 lines — slightly over the 500-line threshold but within acceptable range; modular references and scripts well-utilized

agent_usability.note: Subcommand routing is clear; output columns documented with field descriptions; minor gap: forest-plot required columns may confuse if analyze was run with skip_univariate

human_usability.note: When to Use / When Not to Use sections well-structured with typical user request phrasings; high discoverability

security.note: No credentials or secrets; set.seed prevents side-channel variance; file path inputs accepted without explicit sanitization

maintainability.note: Eight-file modular structure; smoke test script (run_smoke_test.R) and shell wrapper included; test data bundled; references documented

agent_specific.note: Trigger description precise with NOT-for exclusions; progressive disclosure excellent; escape hatches clearly stated; minor composability friction from xlsx output format dependency

key_strengths: Three-subcommand architecture (analyze, forest-plot, multi-forest-plot) cleanly separates analysis from visualization
Automated smoke test script (run_smoke_test.R) and shell wrapper enable easy regression testing
set.seed() present with configurable --seed argument ensuring full reproducibility
Multivariable fallback logic (use all features if fewer than 3 significant) is explicitly documented
When Not to Use section precisely excludes nomograms, calibration, and time-dependent ROC, preventing misuse

recommendations.title: No warning emitted when multivariable fallback triggers
recommendations.problem: When fewer than 3 significant univariate features are found, the skill silently falls back to all requested features. No warning message is documented or confirmed in error handling.
recommendations.root_cause: The fallback logic is implemented but the warning emission to stderr is not confirmed in the code review.
recommendations.fix: Add an explicit log_warn() or cat() message when the fallback triggers, e.g. 'WARNING: Fewer than 3 significant univariate features (N found). Falling back to all requested features for multivariable model.'

recommendations.title: SKILL.md at 327 lines is approaching verbosity threshold
recommendations.problem: The SKILL.md is 327 lines, which is within the 500-line limit but heavier than sibling skills. The Implementation Checklist at the bottom adds 15 lines with limited agent-facing value.
recommendations.root_cause: The Implementation Checklist is a developer artifact that was not removed before publication.
recommendations.fix: Move the Implementation Checklist to a separate CONTRIBUTING.md or remove it from SKILL.md to reduce cognitive load for agent callers.
